metadata.title:
WILL BUILD


|NTERURBAN

Cook-Clark Co. Negotiat-
ing 100000 Develop


ment Proposition.

pages.raw_text:
A deal is on foot whereby the
Cook-Clarke Co. expects to have
built an electric line built from
Spokane to the immense tract
north of the city, between Mead
and hartford, which is recently pur-
chased at a cost of about $500, 900.
The control of this tract, which
contains over 1000 acres, will rest
with the Cassill Investment Co.,
composed of those interested in
the Cooke-Clark Co. and State
Treasurer Cassill of South Dakota.


The plan is to place the entire
tract under irrigation by means of
an immense pumping plant on the
Little Spokane river and sell the
ground in five or 10 acre tracts
and set it under fruit for the pur-
chaser, to be cared for and looked
after by the Clarke Co. until
the trees reach the bearing stage.
To carry out the plans outlined for
the development of the enterprise,
which will bar the title of City
Gardens, will represent an invest
ments of around $1,0O0,00O and
four years of work in the develop
ment and sale of the ground.


The one thing that the enter
prise lacked, transportation, now
seems assured. The manner in
which it is proposed to finance or
promote the building of the new
line to City Gardens is not yet de-
veloped to the point that it can
be given out for publication, but
the men behind the undertaking
say there is no question but that
the line will be built. The dis
lance of City Gardens from How
ard and Riverside varies from
seven to 10 miles.


The electric line power plant
will probably be located on the
Little Spokane.
